title: Chavica guigual
taxonomy: Chavica guigual is a genus of flowering plants in the family Rubiaceae, which consists of a wide range of species, including vines and shrubs. This plant is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The classification of Chavica guigual places it within the larger group of Rubiaceae, a family known for its diverse and complex species. Chavica guigual is part of the order Gentianales, which also includes other flowering plant families such as Apocynaceae and Loganiaceae. Chavica guigual's classification also suggests a close relationship with other members of the Rubiaceae family, many of which have medicinal and ornamental value.
uses: Chavica guigual, also known as the "Mexican peperomia," is a plant with potential medicinal uses.
